articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred intensified over the board, bringing with it a torrent of violent rhetoric against low castes.

The system's leading edge featured calls for beheadings, acid attacks, and other forms of brutal violence, sparking a varied response from the community.

A scattered drizzle of livestream-death gossip persisted in the afternoon, centered around the tragic death of a 24-year-old erotic model during a livestream.

The discussion remained somber, with some posters expressing shock and others seeming to treat the event as a curiosity.

Meanwhile, a cold front of incel anxiety moved into the region, with one poster seeking advice on transforming from incel to 'Chad' in just two months.

The response from the community was sparse, but one respondent did offer some skeptical remarks about the possibility of such a rapid transformation.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Sparking Varied Replies
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post calling for violence against low castes, suggesting they be killed, beheaded, thrown into acid, used as manure, raped, and set on fire.

Another anon responded by blaming low castes for India's poverty and poor reputation, stating they are responsible for ruining prestigious Indian institutions. This poster claimed that low castes, along with poor Indians and scammers, are giving India a bad reputation.

A different anon suggested that allowing low castes to use upper caste (UC) surnames could lead to low castes committing crimes and then blaming upper castes for their actions. This poster mentioned specific cases where this might have occurred.

One anon brought up a case of a dalit man from the mahar caste who was named after Bhimta Rao and was accused of raping a 5-year-old.

This poster noted that the dalit man's community and supporters abandoned him after the crime was exposed.

Another poster countered the OP's claims by pointing out contributions of various minorities to India, including Sikhs in the army, Northeast Indians in football, and Muslims in fields like science, cuisine, and music.

Anon also discussed reservations, with one poster arguing that using reservations is a sign of being 'dysgenic' and leads to 'total dysgenic death.' This poster used derogatory terms to describe those who benefit from reservations.

The thread also saw discussion on the formation of OBC (Other Backward Classes) and its implications, with one poster noting that the creation of OBC was more opposed by upper castes than the reservation for dalits.

Some posters rationalized siding with less 'retarded' and 'dysgenic' individuals within their own community, even if they are considered 'subhuman.' One poster connected epigenetics to the behavior of certain individuals, suggesting a genetic basis for their actions.

articles.title: Erotic Model's Livestream Ends in Death
articles.body:
Anon described a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub erotic gay model who died during a livestream in Italy, amid the COVID-19 pandemic. The model was talking about the latest news while a radio station played in his room.

Anon provided another case of a 24-year-old man from Germany who intentionally caused the death of his 28-year-old gay partner, according to a forensic medicine textbook.

Another anon shared a case of a 23-year-old woman who was doing it with her male partner as a spotter, but he waited too long, leaving her hanging for a minute while he masturbated.

A different scenario involved an 18-year-old streaming on an erotic platform when a viewer requested he masturbate while hanging himself for a donation. The viewer suggested using a bed sheet since the streamer didn't have a rope.

There was debate about whether a similar livestream was a suicide or an accidental death. Some thought the person wanted to make their death stand out by masturbating as they died.

Anon noted that some people get aroused by strange things, like imagining being killed by hanging or strangulation. Others mentioned that a brain deprived of blood can create a hallucinogenic effect.

One respondent shared that they had been watching gore since they were 13, but hanging videos made them uncomfortable. They could imagine the pain.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Incel to Chad Transformation Advice
articles.body:
Anon asked how to achieve an 'Incel to Chad transformation in 2 months.' One respondent suggested taking action rather than seeking advice, telling the OP to 'JUST DO IT. GO TO THE GYM. EAT PROTEIN. SLEEP. GROW A BEARD.

GET A HAIRCUT. STOP FAP. STOP BITCHING. STOP WHINING.

STOP BEING A FUCKING LOSER.' Another anon discussed their personal situation, saying they had lost their virginity at 25 to a woman who was 26, and were concerned about not having enough money to support a family.

This poster seemed to seek advice on how to navigate their current relationship and financial situation.

A different approach was offered by an anon who suggested that the OP should stop using labels like 'randis' and instead think of promiscuous women as simply that, as it 'enhances your chances of banging one if you scale.' This poster positioned this as a step towards 'chadhood.' One respondent jokingly suggested getting a vasectomy, saying it's 'not worth having a nanhajeet in this economy.' This poster claimed governments want population growth to maintain a large workforce.

Conversations also touched on underwear preferences, with one poster distinguishing between thongs and G-strings. Anon advice ranged from practical self-improvement to flippant dismissal.

articles.title: Anon derides Buddhism, praises Hinduism
articles.body:
Anon questioned the intelligence of Buddhists, calling them 'atheist' and suggesting their religion promotes 'cuckery.' They claimed Hinduism is superior, citing its acceptance of activities like smoking and drinking.

Anon responded, saying Buddhism emerged from a single person and was later adopted by non-Indians as a better religion.

Another poster argued that actual Buddhist scholars do not recognize Ambedkarite Buddhism as a legitimate form of the faith. Anon also claimed Buddhism operates like parasitism and relies on moral high ground.

Others discussed the conversion of Dalits to Buddhism and its impact on Hinduism. The conversation included inflammatory and derogatory comments about various groups.

articles.title: Indians on Pakistan's Existence
articles.body:
Anon posted that if Pakistan vanished, Indian patriotic zeal would plummet. Anon responded, saying Indians do not obsess over Pakistan and have better things to do.

Another anon disagreed, stating coexistence with Islam is not possible. Anon claimed low castes and Muslims are enemies of Aryans. A respondent agreed Islam is the problem but urged Aryans to follow Vedic dharma.

Anon also mentioned Pakistan's military capabilities and treatment of low castes.
